name: SynSync
subtitle: Open-source neuroacoustic entrainment
thesis: Neuroacoustic tools remain understandable and user-directed when sound is designed as a calibrated interface — not passive ambience — and every claim is graded by the evidence behind it.
description: SynSync is an open-source, local-first brainwave entrainment platform. It generates binaural and isochronic acoustic protocols for sleep, focus, calm, energy, mood, and creativity — each graded by an explicit evidence level (I–IV) with cited peer-reviewed research. Runs entirely in-browser, no account, no data leaves the device.
before: Wellness audio is passive ambience with unverifiable claims.
after: Sound becomes a transparent, evidence-graded state interface the user controls.

levels.I: Strongest Evidence — Multiple high-quality studies with consistent findings
levels.II: Strong Evidence — Several peer-reviewed studies supporting this protocol
levels.III: Moderate Evidence — Preliminary research with promising results
levels.IV: Emerging Evidence — Limited studies, requires further validation

sample_citations: Marshall, L. et al. (2006) "Boosting slow oscillations during sleep." Nature
Lutz, A. et al. (2004) "Long-term meditators self-induce high-amplitude gamma synchrony."
Chaieb, L. et al. (2015) "Auditory Beat Stimulation and Mood States." Front Psychiatry
Padmanabhan, R. et al. (2005) "Pre-operative anxiety reduction with 6Hz entrainment."
evidence_model.technical: 48kHz/24-bit render, octave layers, warm carrier, pink noise, spatial rotation, binaural + isochronic modes

proof.for: Individuals seeking focus, rest, mood, and recovery support
proof.limit: Entrainment is supportive, not medical treatment; claims are evidence-graded, not certified

inv.human: Use a real, open-source system for attention and rest today — no account, no tracking.
inv.builder: Embeddable entrainment engine with evidence-graded protocol library.
inv.researcher: Acoustic entrainment with cited literature and explicit evidence levels.
